introduction: This refers to the clinical, physiological and biochemical manifestation of the effect of excess thyroid hormone on tissues. There is a female preponderance due to the role of autoimmunity in the aetiology.

features: A goitre may or may not be present
Triad of Proptosis, Nail changes (onycholyis) and Skin changes strongly suggest Graves' disease.

investigations: Thyroid Function Test with free T4 and T3 (both high, TSH suppressed)
Thyroid antibodies: Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies, Thyroglobulin antibody, TSH Receptor antibodies

goals: Achieve normal metabolic rate by attaining normal serum T3, T4 and TSH levels
drug: Carbimazole: Adult starting dose 30 - 60 mg orally in divided doses daily; Maintenance 10 - 15 mg orally, daily
Propylthiouracil (preferred in pregnancy): Adult starting dose 300 - 450 mg orally in divided doses daily; Maintenance 100 - 150 mg orally in 2 or 3 divided doses daily
Beta-adrenergic blocking drugs: Propranolol 80 - 160 mg orally, daily in divided doses
Iodine (Lugol's solution) in emergency management of thyroid storm, preoperative preparation, etc.
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Carbimazole and propylthiouracil may cause severe bone marrow suppression (including pancytopenia and agranulocytosis)
supportive_measures: Appropriate care of any organ or system involved e.g. eye care, and treatment of heart failure
Thyroid storm would require judicious intravenous fluid use, corticosteroids and treatment of the precipitating cause

definitive_treatment: Surgery indicated for patients < 21 years, those intolerant to other agents, patients with large goitres, toxic adenomas, and multinodular goitres
